_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in identifying and anticipating system/server performance, availability, capacity, or configuration problems.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in diagnosing and resolving ML infrastructure failures, including training job crashes, serving latency spikes, resource contention across shared clusters, and data pipeline breakdowns that affect model inputs.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-017 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.591), TF-T-007 (TF, cosine: 0.520).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.492). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [anticipating, availability, capacity, configuration, identifying, performance] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, affect, breakdowns, clusters, contention, crashes] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.897, R=0.869). P exceeds R by 0.029 — the DCWF element's 12 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 28 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Skill in identifying and anticipating system/server performance, availability, c" and "Skill in diagnosing and resolving ML infrastructure failures, including training" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.600 > STS 0.492 (gap +0.108). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.1%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [anticipating, availability, capacity, configuration, identifying, performance]. WIDAI-only: [across, affect, breakdowns, clusters, contention, crashes].
